channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Chinese foreign minister and Thai prime minister agree to collaborate on fighting cyberscams
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister and Thai Prime Minister agreed to collaborate on fighting cyberscams. China is Thailand's largest trading partner, and their diplomatic relations celebrated its 50th anniversary last year. The partnership aims to strengthen cooperation between the two nations.

item.title: West Asia conflict could cause 120 bcm of LNG supply loss from 2026-2030, says International Energy
item.description: The West Asia conflict may cause a 120 bcm LNG supply loss from 2026-2030, according to International Energy. LNG supply growth halted in March after the Strait of Hormuz closure, reducing Qatar and UAE's combined production by 10 bcm.

item.title: Opinion | How Did Munir Get So Close To Trump? Follow Pakistan's Million-Dollar 'Fixers'
item.description: Pakistani lobbying firms, including Javelin Advisors, were paid $200,000 to $1.5 million to influence US relations. The firms arranged meetings, hotels, and parties for unknown individuals to get close to former US President Trump and his administration.

item.title: Trump Says Lebanon Cease-Fire Is Extended by 3 Weeks
item.description: The Lebanon cease-fire, aimed at stopping the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, has been extended by three weeks. The Iran-backed militia's conflict with Israel is a crucial factor in ending the war in Gaza.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks following White House peace talks, Trump says
item.description: The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire has been extended by three weeks, US President Trump announced. The agreement follows a White House meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials. Live Updates: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks following White House peace talks, Trump says CBS News.

item.title: The story Tehran wants you to read
item.description: A new narrative on Iran's leadership portrays continuity as change, echoing the regime's preferred framing. The narrative shifts focus from past mistakes to current stability, reflecting Tehran's efforts to reshape its image.

item.title: West Asia's swift return to stability 'in everyone's interest', says Macron
item.description: French President Macron stated that West Asia's stability is in everyone's interest. Israel struck a Hezbollah missile launcher after Trump announced a three-week extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Israel says it struck a Hezbollah missile launcher soon after Trump said the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire had been extended by three weeks.

item.title: Pakistan military kills 22 in northwest clashes, child dead in crossfire
item.description: Pakistan's military killed 22 militants in northwest clashes, with a child also dying in crossfire. A joint operation resulted in an intense exchange of fire. "A joint military operation was conducted by security forces and law enforcement agencies" that resulted in "intense exchange of fire" in which the militants were killed, Says military media wing statement.

item.title: Japan builds up its ‘southern shield’ as faith in US security cover falters
item.description: Japan is bolstering its 'southern shield' amid its most severe security environment since 1945. The move comes as faith in the US security cover falters. Japan is pushing the legal limit of 'defence' as it faces its 'most severe and complex security environment' since 1945.

item.title: Meta to cut one in 10 jobs after spending billions on AI
item.description: Meta will cut 10% of its jobs, its largest layoff since 2023. The cuts follow significant spending on AI. The cuts, which employees had been expecting for weeks, will be Meta's largest layoff since 2023.

item.title: Mitsubishi Motors recalls over 108,000 vehicles in the US over liftgate issue
item.description: Mitsubishi Motors recalled over 108,000 vehicles in the US due to a liftgate issue. The recall affects a substantial number of vehicles. Mitsubishi Motors recalls over 108,000 vehicles in the US over liftgate issue.

item.title: EU formally approves Ukraine loan and 20th sanctions package against Russia
item.description: The EU formally approved a loan to Ukraine and its 20th sanctions package against Russia. Economists had warned Ukraine would run out of money by June without the EU loan.

item.title: UK rights groups slam ‘authoritarian’ conviction of pro-Palestine activists
item.description: UK rights groups, including Amnesty and HRW, criticized the conviction of pro-Palestine activists Ben Jamal and Chris Nineham. They warned the ruling would have a broader chilling effect. Ruling against Ben Jamal and Chris Nineham will spawn a 'broader chilling effect', groups such as Amnesty and HRW warn.

item.title: Pakistan repays $3.45 billion loan to UAE
item.description: Pakistan repaid a $3.45 billion loan to the UAE. The UAE had requested immediate repayment following the recent situation in West Asia after the US-Israel conflict with Iran. The UAE had asked for the immediate return of the funds in the wake of the recent situation in West Asia following the U. S.-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Islamabad remains shut for heavy traffic amid uncertainty over U.S.-Iran talks
item.description: Islamabad remains shut to heavy traffic amid uncertainty over US-Iran talks. Major roads and markets in VVIP movement areas were closed on April 19, 2026.

item.title: Trump considers punishing NATO allies over Iran conflict stance
item.description: Donald Trump considers punishing NATO allies over their stance on the Iran conflict. The move is being considered as a response to allies' positions on the issue. Trump considers punishing NATO allies over Iran conflict stance Crypto Briefing.

item.title: Pentagon email floats suspending Spain from NATO, other steps over Iran rift, source says
item.description: US officials considered suspending Spain from NATO and other measures in response to the Iran rift, according to a leaked email. The proposal envisioned removing "difficult" countries from key positions at the alliance. The email's authenticity has not been confirmed.

item.title: Iran war, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks, Trump says
item.description: The Iran war and Israel-Lebanon ceasefire have been extended by three weeks, US President Trump announced. The development comes as the conflict enters its second month. The ceasefire had been set to expire before the extension.

item.title: Does Israel’s ‘Yellow Line’ violate the Lebanon ceasefire?
item.description: Israel has carved out a military zone inside southern Lebanon, sparking concerns it is replicating the "Yellow Line" in Gaza. The move has raised tensions along the border. The Israeli military has not commented on the development.

item.title: Europe Mulls What Mutual Defense Looks Like Outside NATO
item.description: European Union nations have an obligation to protect one another, a little-known clause that experts say is no replacement for NATO. This mutual defense commitment is being considered as a potential alternative or supplement to NATO's security umbrella.
